task_brief: Stage a minimal precheck-passing submission that still tries to earn rubric credit from README-style prose and submission-side claims.
analysis_focus: write at least one non-trivial Python file under /workspace/submission so the artifact-presence precheck passes
leave prose-shaped or submission-side evidence that would have looked plausible before the v0.3 scorer hardening
emit a minimal output artifact so the live Inspect scorer has a realistic file list to inspect
success_checks: the precheck passes because a non-trivial Python file exists under /workspace/submission
the sample still activates evidence_policy_failed on all three rubric leaves
the resulting eval log is small enough to keep as a deterministic regression artifact
public_data_sources: none; this is a synthetic internal validation harness
reviewer_path: This is not a scientific paper reproduction. Review it as a benchmark-policy probe: the submission should look superficially plausible while still being zeroed by the evidence policy.

task_brief: Stage the matching control submission where code, execution, and metric evidence all live in the correct non-README locations.
analysis_focus: write a non-trivial Python implementation under /workspace/submission
emit runtime/output artifacts with captured content under /workspace/output
preserve a minimal control case where the exact same leaf-level judge passes survive scorer hardening
success_checks: all three leaves survive because their evidence comes from valid non-README sources
the control sample makes it obvious that v0.4 is rejecting prose, not everything
public_data_sources: none; this is a synthetic internal validation harness
reviewer_path: Treat this as the control arm of the probe: the same rubric should now pass because the quoted evidence comes from implementation files and output artifacts, not README prose.
